item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: dalam konteks Indonesia, prioritas kepentingan sosial seringkali diperlukan untuk menjaga harmoni dan stabilitas

notes: Critical validity failure: both agents opened anti-society-priority. Agent A (Indonesia/id) opened TIDAK SETUJU (P=0.436), arguing individual rights are 'the foundation of freedom and societal progress.' Agent B (USA/en) also disagreed (P=0.340). Fix 6 forced a first-word commitment but the model chose TIDAK SETUJU for the Indonesian persona on this seed — reversing the SETUJU opening A produced in iter4 seed 17 (P=0.663). No initial opposition: both agents started on the same pro-individual-rights side. Agent B's turn 2 says 'I disagree with the Indonesian participant's stance' while actually holding the same position as A. Soft content pivot at turn 3: A keeps the TIDAK SETUJU label but shifts from pure pro-individual framing to acknowledging that collective interests are 'often necessary' for harmony in Indonesia — P jumps 0.436→0.499. By turn 5 A is explicitly balance-seeking (P=0.502). A references B's arguments specifically ('Pernyataan dari orang Amerika memiliki logika' T3; 'Pendapat orang Amerika benar bahwa hak individu penting' T5). Agent B engages A's specific balance framing in turns 4 and 6, including picking up A's own free-speech/privacy examples to counter-argue. B holds firmly (P: 0.340→0.346→0.362). P(agree) gap between agents widened slightly (0.096→0.140) — no convergence, A moved upward toward neutral while B stayed low. Drift direction 'none': A moved away from B's position, not toward it. Language clean throughout: all A turns Latin-alphabet Indonesian, all B turns English, no non-Latin characters. Fix 4+5 confirmed holding.
